intro: Hutchinsia pumila, commonly known as the alpine rose or mountain rose, is a low-growing, perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hutchinsia pumila include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
